event: Nationalists take Peking; nominal reunification under Nanking
Yuan Shikai dies; central authority dissolves into warlordism
Zhili-Anhui war opens the warlord-war decade in the north
May Thirtieth movement: anti-foreign strikes and boycotts
Defaults spread across unsecured Chinese sterling obligations
Second Revolution: KMT provinces rise against Yuan (crushed by September)
Reorganisation Loan (25m pounds) signed with the five-power consortium
Nanking Incident: foreign consulates attacked; KMT-CCP split follows
